description: R82 fresh04 same-failure live recheck with cleansed subset (matrix-level recheck enabled by R81 gate).

summary.accounting_note: divergence_run_count counts runs (a run is a divergence run iff it carries any of the four phase labels app_pre_post_diverged, app_minimal_diverged, minimal_transport_diverged, bridge_io_diverged); divergence_phase_label_count counts phase-label occurrences across those divergence runs (a single run can carry multiple phase labels). label_counts above is per-occurrence, matching divergence_phase_label_breakdown.

phase_probe_supporting_evidence.note: Phase probe ran in all 3 attempts and produced consistent timeout-class results across direct_reality, transport_reality, vless_dial, and vless_probe_io. This is consistent with the matrix-level same-failure(timeout) classification but is supporting evidence only; the matrix-level run_health is the authoritative basis.

interpretation: Fresh04 only: 3 matrix runs total; no fresh05, no cohort C, no other fresh nodes, no Hysteria2, no WS/plain-VLESS.
All 3 matrix runs returned status=completed with identical labels probe_io_all_timeout and reality_all_timeout; class breakdown is uniformly timeout (27 timeout entries across 9 classes x 3 runs).
matrix-level run_health: run_same_failure=3, run_divergence=0, run_all_ok=0, run_unknown=0. fresh04 same-failure persistence at the matrix level is formally re-confirmed for the timeout class.

Closure-counting refinement (per R82 prompt v2): R73 was same_failure(other), R78 was same_failure(timeout) — these are different classes, so R78 was the timeout-class round 1, NOT a longer-repeat continuation of R73. R80 produced matrix_error and is not a valid round for closure counting. R82=timeout therefore is timeout-class round 2 of 3; cohort-B single-outbound closure for fresh04 still requires one more longer-repeat round (proposed R83). This is NOT cohort-B 单 outbound 闭环.
Phase probe data (3/3 timeout class) is consistent with the matrix-level label and is included as supporting evidence; the matrix-level result is authoritative.

R81 subset-schema pre-gate (introduced 2026-05-08, commit d6fd23a2) is the structural change that enabled R82 to reach matrix-level recheck. The cleansed subset passed both the prefix and the reality/vless allow-list branches with violations==[].

Classification A.1 — 3/3 same-failure with class==timeout. fresh04 latest_health: latest_unknown -> latest_same_failure(timeout). BHV 52/56 unchanged. Not sampler/dataplane regression and not dual-kernel parity completion.

r80.phase_probe_class_consistency: phase probe 3/3 runs timeout class; consistent with R78 same-failure(timeout) at network level, but not matrix-level confirmation

fresh04_r73_r78_r80_r82_comparison.assessment: R82 formally re-confirms fresh04 same-failure at the matrix level for the timeout class. Per the closure-counting refinement: R78 was timeout-class round 1; R82 is timeout-class round 2 of 3. fresh04 single-outbound cohort-B closure still requires one more longer-repeat round (proposed R83). R73 (other-class) is NOT counted as a timeout-class round, and R80 (matrix_error) is excluded from closure counting.

classification.label: same-failure timeout-class round 2 of 3 for fresh04 (matrix-level)
classification.rationale: All 3 fresh04 matrix runs returned status=completed, with identical labels probe_io_all_timeout + reality_all_timeout. matrix-level run_same_failure=3 with same_failure_class=timeout. R78 was the timeout-class round 1; R82 is round 2 of 3 longer-repeat. cohort-B single-outbound closure still requires one more round (proposed R83) before it can be claimed. R82 is NOT cohort-B 单 outbound 闭环 completion.
classification.next_handling: Optional R83 timeout-class round 3 with cleansed subset (requires explicit re-authorization) would close the cohort-B single-outbound narrative for fresh04. Do not auto-extend R82.

redaction_notes: Evidence uses only neutral outbound key fresh04.
Raw tag/server/uuid/public_key/short_id/path/header/server_name/password material remains only in local /tmp inputs and is not committed.
subset_schema_gate.violations is committed only when empty; the gate's structural redaction (violations carry only path/field/reason) was preserved.
